title: Railways contributed immensely to nation’s overall development: Gehlot
content: Governor of Karnataka Thawar Chand Gehlot has said that the Indian Railways had not only strengthened the Indian economy but also had contributed immensely to the overall development of the country.

title: Prime Minister virtually inaugurates redeveloped Koppal railway station under Amrit Station Scheme
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi virtually inaugurated the redeveloped Koppal station under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme

title: Another case of minor girl getting pregnant comes to light in Dharwad
content: Close on the heels of a minor girl delivering a baby, another case of minor girl getting pregnant because of alleged sexual exploitation has come to light in Dharwad district.

title: Special team to conduct detailed probe into Dharani transactions
content: The government had already ordered a audit which probed transactions in Siddipet and Medak districts on a pilot basis, and the exercise was extended to the remaining parts of State; Cabinet takes the decision following spate of complaints like inclusion of vast lands in prohibited list

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Opposition support grows as Wangchuk’s hunger strike continues on day 20
content: “The medical team stated that Sonam Wangchuk continues to be under 24-hour medical vigilance, with his health being monitored closely,” the CJP said

title: Sajad Lone, Altaf Bukhari will not join NC’s Statehood protest in Delhi; Congress to take part
content: While Sajad Lone questioned both the timing and real motive, J&K Apni Party chief Altaf Bukhari said the NC’s proposed Jantar Mantar protest “is to empower its government not to empower people”

title: India’s electoral roll is a living document: CEC
content: The highest-ever poll participation in the recent Assembly elections is a proof of the trust the electors have in the country’s electoral system and the vibrancy of the  democracy, says Chief Election Commissioner

title: Opposition support grows as Wangchuk’s hunger strike continues on day 20
content: The protest seeking Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation attracted more high-profile visitors

title: Mandatory training for staff to make hospitals patient-friendly
content: Decision to make soft skills training mandatory for hospital employees was taken in  the backdrop of innumerable  complaints from patients and visitors regarding poor behaviour by hospital staff, communication lapses, and unsatisfactory interactions with the public, says Health Minister

title: Govt. to launch bullet rail project with Indian train; Japan to provide next-gen Shinkansen series after 4 years
content: Officials say work on Mumbai-Ahmedabad high-speed rail project progressing well, they reject former Japanese Minister’s remarks

title: HC grants interim bail to Sub-Registrar Justin Manikandan Subramanian in the Palani math fraudulent land registration case
content: Justice K. Rajasekar directed the petitioner to report before the CB-CID at 10.30 a.m. every day from July 27 till August 3, except on Sunday, and co-operate in the investigation.  The larger conspiracy behind the registration of the document was still under investigation and the petitioner was involved in two previous criminal cases of a similar nature, says the State government

title: One more U.S. made anti-submarine helicopter delivered to the Indian Navy
content: The MH-60R is among the world’s most advanced shipborne helicopters; it is designed primarily for anti-submarine and anti-surface warfare

title: Fearing fake certificates, Centre orders stricter scrutiny of digitised birth and death records
content: Complaints regarding issuance of fake/unauthorised digitised certificates in some instances not only undermines the integrity of the Civil Registration System but also threatens the reliability of vital records, says Deputy Registrar General in letter to States

title: Government brings major changes in the weightage of marks for teacher recruitments in Karnataka
content: The government given more importance to the competitive exam score rather than the marks obtained in B.Ed., B.P.Ed and D.Ed. Courses.

title: Shashi Tharoor rescued after being trapped in hotel lift
content: Using a hydraulic spreader, the firefighters managed to open the lift and safely rescue the MP and other passengers who were trapped for nearly 30 minutes

title: PM launches country’s first hydrogen train, takes jibe at Congress and AAP
content: Mr. Modi says had “Hormuz” situation occurred before 2014, the Indian Railways would have come to a standstill as trains in most of the country ran solely on diesel; he points out that nearly 99% of India’s railway network has been electrified in the last 12 years; PM lashes out at AAP leaders for indulging in ‘extortion, illegal collection, and unauthorised possessions’ as part of their political culture

title: Widow’s loss prompts SC push to fix overcrowded trains
content: Orders railway to pay ₹8 lakh compensation to a woman whose husband died after falling from a moving train in November 2015.

title: NCP (SP) to join NDA only as part of a ‘unified NCP’: BJP tells Mahayuti allies
content: According to a senior office bearer of the BJP privy to the matter, the BJP high command is very clear that it does not want any insecurity among its current allies — Shinde-led Shiv Sena and the NCP — on their position within the NDA bloc

title: India’s first private orbital rocket Vikram-1 set for lift-off
content: Vikram-1’s flight sequence, from lift-off to orbit injection, will cover 14 phases, and the total duration of the flight will be 15.46 minutes

title: Rahul Gandhi calls for political consensus to end the ‘paper-leak industry’
content: The Leader of Opposition advocated a comprehensive overhaul by introducing randomised question papers and ensuring punishment for those who leak papers

title: Shia body urges govt. to secure release of detained Indian Muslims in Gulf countries
content: All India Shia Personal Law Board general secretary Maulana Yasoob Abbas says the Board had submitted a memorandum to PM Narendra Modi, requesting the Centre to engage diplomatically with the governments of the countries concerned and ensure their safety

title: Congress’s Karti Chidambaram says the proposal to expand Lok Sabha to 815 seats will make the House ‘ineffective’
content: The Congress MP  urged the Centre to consult all political parties before taking any decision on the issue, alleging that the government could “hide behind technicalities and procedures” to avoid wider consultations

title: SIT arrests second man accused of ‘pocketing Badrinath temple donations’
content: The primary FIR was registered based on a complaint by the BKTC president alleging financial irregularities in the counting and handling of cash and other offerings made by devotees at the shrine

title: ISL star swaps jersey for writer’s pen in maiden travelogue
content: Oru Panthukalikkarante Indian Yathrakal, which features a cover unveiled by Indian football legend Sunil Chhetri on July 17, chronicles the experiences of celebrated footballer C.K. Vineeth’s  63-day solo expedition across India

title: Experts call for responsible AI at VIT-AP conference
content: Industry leaders and researchers highlight ethical innovation, real-world applications and collaboration to advance Artificial Intelligence research

title: Civilian ‘dies of gunshot wounds’ in J&K’s Bhaderwah; Internet suspended in Chenab Valley areas
content: Deceased’s wife joins protests; hundreds of locals assemble in Bhaderwah and condemn “killing of innocent civilian”

title: Centre responds positively to five key cultural proposals, says Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: Circular soon on National School of Drama in Rajamahendravaram, Indian Council for Cultural Relations centre in State, Lalit Kala Akademi, Kalakshetra Auditorium in Vijayawada, and Sahitya Akademi centre in Kadapa, he says

title: How satellite maps are leading fishermen to bigger catches
content: The Potential Fishing Zone advisory, developed by Hyderabad-based INCOIS, has benefited nearly 14 lakh fishermen over past two decades by using satellite data to identify waters where fish are likely to aggregate; the system is now evolving beyond improving catches to promoting sustainable fishing through species-specific advisories and ecological safeguards

title: Naidu seeks Nirmala Sitharaman’s support for setting up of Indian School of Agriculture in A.P.
content: Chief Minister also seeks Central government's intervention to stabilise prices of raw materials used in the manufacture of shrimp feed as steep hike is adversely impacting business

title: Oceansat-3A likely to be launched by year-end, says NRSC Director
content: Coastal observation research laboratory inaugurated at Andhra University’s Centre for Studies on Bay of Bengal

title: Baptism of Yamal by 'Saint' Messi: The photo that became WC final's origin story
content: Made in La Masia:  Lamine Yamal vs Lionel Messi. How a photograph captures the unlikely meeting of two of Barcelona's most famous graduates. Read more in today's Offside.

title: 'A true icon of the game': BCCI pays tribute to Garry Sobers with throwback video
content: The BCCI honoured Sir Garfield Sobers, a true cricket icon, after his passing. He was remembered as one of the game's greatest all-rounders ever. Sobers left an enduring legacy which will continue to inspire many. Cricket West Indies also posted a heartfelt farewell to the legend. His remarkable career redefined greatness in the sport.

title: US may add $100,000 bond to Green Card applicants seeking permanent residence
content: The US government is considering a $100,000 bond for Green Card applicants. This measure aims to ensure immigrants are financially self-sufficient and do not burden public programs. The Trump administration is exploring this long-standing legal authority under the Immigration and Nationality Act. This proposal follows earlier attempts to impose fees on H-1B visa applications. Officials stated this policy reduced overstays in pilot programs.

title: Techie hangs self; family takes body to boyfriend's village, seeks posthumous wedding
content: A 24-year-old software engineer from Andhra Pradesh allegedly died by suicide in her Mumbai PG after her boyfriend reportedly refused to marry her. Her family took her body to his village in Prakasam district, demanding he fulfil her alleged last wish by tying a mangalsutra before cremation. The protest triggered tension before police intervened and the last rites were conducted.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: How 58 beavers revived a valley buried by Mount St. Helens eruption
content: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.

title: Apple is world's most valuable company again: How AI is the reason for Nvidia's 'loss'
content: Apple has reclaimed its position as the world's most valuable company.  This shift reflects investor focus moving beyond initial AI hardware gains.  Investors are now prioritizing memory chips and data center infrastructure development.  Nvidia experienced a significant stock price decline, losing its top valuation.  Apple's recent surge is attributed to its consumer ecosystem and AI advancements.

title: Delhi cab driver dies after iron rod from overloaded e-rickshaw tears through windshield
content: A cab driver was killed and a woman passenger seriously injured after an iron rod protruding from an e-rickshaw pierced the cab’s windshield and struck the driver during a collision on the Dwarka Expressway in Gurugram on Friday. Police suspect overspeeding and are examining CCTV footage to determine the sequence of events. The e-rickshaw driver has been detained and an investigation is underway.

title: SLC breaks silence after Manjot Kalra's arrest, vows zero tolerance for corruption
content: Sri Lanka Cricket reaffirmed its zero-tolerance policy on corruption following an arrest. Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra faces allegations of corrupt practice during the LPL. The ongoing investigation will not impact the tournament's scheduled continuation. Sri Lanka Cricket pledged full cooperation with the police special investigation unit. Robust anti-corruption measures remain in place to protect the league's integrity.

title: Connor McDavid trade rumor: Flyers emerge as potential landing spot for Oilers star
content: Connor McDavid is back in the center of NHL trade rumors, this time because former NHL player Todd Fedoruk thinks the Philadelphia Flyers could be a possible destination. The comments have opened the debate about the long-term future of the Edmonton Oilers captain despite his contract and no-movement clause, even if there are no confirmed trade talks.

title: Erling Haaland's net worth soars after 2026 World Cup as global fame boosts fortune
content: Erling Haaland heads into the post-2026 World Cup period with a net worth of approximately $100 million, backed by $80 million in annual earnings, a $30 million real estate portfolio, and brand deals with Nike, Breitling, and Beats by Dre. Forbes ranks him the fourth highest-paid player at the tournament. The World Cup supercharged his social media presence, with nearly 29 million new Instagram followers in six weeks, pushing his audience past 69 million and raising his estimated earnings per sponsored post to roughly $695,900. At 25, his financial ceiling is still rising.

title: Blake Lively left heartbroken as Gigi Hadid reportedly deepens Taylor Swift rift
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce kept the drama away from their New York City wedding as they invited pretty much everyone from Hollywood and the NFL but Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, sparking chaos among many. While this has sparked a mixed reaction among fans, it seems supermodel Gigi Hadid might have made things worse for Blake Lively.

title: US-based Hindu rights group backs Sonam's hunger strike, plans Washington rally
content: Hindus for Human Rights will gather in Washington supporting Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Wangchuk has been fasting for twenty days demanding the education minister's resignation. The advocacy group expressed concern over Wangchuk's deteriorating health and student hospitalizations. They highlighted the spiritual significance of fasting as a moral protest. The organization urged the government to engage with protesters before a medical emergency.

title: West Indies cricket legend Garry Sobers dies at 89
content: Cricket icon Garfield Sobers has passed away at the age of 89. He represented the West Indies in 93 Test matches during his career. Sobers scored 8,032 runs and took 235 wickets in Tests. He famously scored 365 runs in a single Test innings. Sobers also achieved six sixes in one over in first-class cricket.

title: 1 dead, 3 injured after chemical tanker explodes in Mumbai’s Chembur
content: A chemical tanker exploded during maintenance work in Chembur, Mumbai on Friday.  Three individuals sustained injuries in the blast, with one later succumbing to their wounds.  Emergency services and firefighters quickly responded to the scene following the incident.  Rescue and response operations involved multiple civic and ambulance authorities.  The exact cause of the explosion is currently under investigation by officials.

title: NRI reveals 5 things nobody warned her about working in India
content: An NRI's post detailing Indian office culture challenges has resonated widely online.  Many professionals shared similar experiences after returning to India from abroad.  Common issues include meeting delays and a different sense of workplace urgency.  Observations also touched upon office dynamics and feedback reception among colleagues.  These shared experiences highlight a familiar reality for many Indian workers.

title: Trump alleges China interference amid suspicion he's prepping for mid-term loss
content: President Donald Trump alleged China compromised American election data extensively. He claimed Beijing illicitly acquired millions of US voter files. Critics questioned these claims, citing public data availability and intelligence assessments. Intelligence agencies concluded China did not attempt to alter vote counts. The timing of these allegations raised concerns among Republicans and Democrats.

title: Gujarat ATS arrests 5 more in Jaish module; probe reveals 8 IED trial runs
content: The Gujarat ATS has apprehended five additional suspects connected to a Jaish-e-Mohammed module, uncovered while they were preparing for explosive device attacks. This group had a history of recruitment and radicalization, engaging in bomb-making training for several years. Investigators reported that they executed at least eight trial runs for explosive timers, and the ongoing investigation aims to unveil potential targets and their external affiliations.

title: Which are India's most investor-friendly states? Gujarat tops Niti Aayog index
content: The index evaluates states and UTs across eight key pillars: infrastructure, business climate, resources, government policy, regulatory ease, institutional environment, financial health and environmental resilience.

title: NEET aspirant jumps to death from Faridabad highrise after calculating probable score
content: Tragedy struck in Faridabad as an 18-year-old NEET aspirant took her own life on Thursday. After reviewing her probable score, which she calculated to be approximately 600 out of 720, she allegedly leapt from the 16th floor. Distressed over her potential results, family members noted concerns leading up to the tragic event, prompting an ongoing police investigation into the circumstances surrounding her death.

title: Delimitation debate: How opposition faultlines may help govt in Monsoon session
content: There are strong speculations that the government is planning to reintroduce the Delimitation Bill during upcoming Monsoon Session. The Bill could not pass the numbers test during the Budget session. Opposition parties, which were united in their opposition to the Bill in April, appear to be divided now over the idea of uniform seat increase.  Some parties may support the bill if their concerns are addressed.  The Congress fears isolation and has requested an all-party meeting for discussions.  The proposed 50% seat increase aims to facilitate women's reservation without altering current proportions.

title: FIFA World Cup final frenzy: Maharashtra hotels push to keep bars open till 3.30am
content: Maharashtra's hospitality industry seeks extended bar operating hours for the FIFA World Cup Final.  The association requested permission to remain open until 3:30 AM on July 20.  This move aims to provide a safe environment for fans watching the global sporting event.  Other states have granted similar one-time relaxations during past World Cup finals.  The government's decision will impact fan arrangements and the hospitality sector's business.

title: FIFA WC final in danger? Wildfire haze engulfs New Jersey as Spain train through smoke
content: Wildfire smoke has created unhealthy air quality across New York and New Jersey.  The FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain is scheduled for Sunday.  Organisers are monitoring conditions closely for potential weather disruptions.  Spain trained outdoors despite hazardous air quality levels on Thursday.  Forecasts suggest improving conditions and rainfall may clear the smoke before the match.

title: Apple overtakes Nvidia to become world's most valuable company again
content: Apple Inc. has regained its position as the world's most valuable company. Nvidia's stock experienced a sharp decline, reducing its market capitalization significantly. Investors are now favoring companies with strong long-term growth prospects over AI boom beneficiaries. Apple's recent share surge and China's approval for Apple Intelligence support its valuation. HSBC upgraded Apple's stock, citing an improving operational outlook for the company.

title: 'Colonial mindset endures': Mehbooba slams Centre over silence on Wangchuk's fast
content: PDP chief Mehbooba Mufti criticized the government's silence on Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike.  Wangchuk has been on an indefinite hunger strike for twenty days demanding action.  He is protesting alleged irregularities in the NEET examination and paper leaks.  Doctors have warned of critical health risks if the hunger strike continues.  Wangchuk vows to continue his protest until July twentieth for a government response.

title: 'Kattar Beimaan Party': PM Modi attacks AAP at Jalandhar rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi criticised Punjab's ruling Aam Aadmi Party for corruption allegations. He labeled the party "Kattar Beimaan Party" during a rally in Jalandhar. The prime minister also highlighted the state's deteriorating law and order situation. He mentioned a former AAP leader's conviction for murder during Delhi riots. Modi urged voters to elect the BJP for a prosperous Punjab.

title: Stranger than fiction: Messi, Yamal set for WC final 19 years after first meeting
content: Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal will face each other in the World Cup final.  Nineteen years ago, Messi bathed baby Yamal during a charity photoshoot event.  Yamal has since become Spain's biggest football star at Barcelona.  Messi left Barcelona in 2021, while Yamal emerged as a global superstar.  This remarkable full-circle moment culminates in Sunday's biggest football match.

title: RIL Q2 FY27: Profit falls 22% after Asian Paints gain; revenue rises 25% YoY
content: RIL results: Revenue from operations rose 25% year-on-year to Rs 3.11 lakh crore in the June quarter, compared with Rs 2.48 lakh crore in the same quarter last year. Quarterly EBITDA increased 10% from a year earlier to Rs 54,067 crore.

title: Hormuz closure: Why crude oil prices haven’t spiralled out of control
content: Brent crude prices in the spot market briefly surged to around $144 per barrel before easing as markets adjusted. Yet as ADB notes in its report - despite this unprecedented supply shock, crude oil prices did not reach levels seen during the 1973 Arab oil embargo or even the 1990 Gulf war, if one were to inflation-adjust the prices.

title: Spanish PM, King to attend World Cup final as Argentine President stays home
content: Spain's Prime Minister and King will attend the World Cup final match.  US President Donald Trump also confirmed his attendance at the New York stadium.  Argentina's President Javier Milei will watch from home due to superstition.  He believes his presence at home brings Argentina luck in matches.  This decision follows a tradition of avoiding presidential attendance at crucial games.

title: CM Vijay inspects adi dravidar hostel in Chennai; students tell him about worms in food
content: Several students preparing for TNPSC and UPSC examinations said the hostel gates were shut by 9.30pm, forcing those returning late from work or coaching classes to sleep outside, including in nearby parks.

title: Oscar award winning actor Brenda Fricker of 'Home Alone 2' fame passes away at 81
content: Brenda Fricker, the acclaimed Irish actor, has passed away at the age of eighty-one. She was the first Irish actor to win an Academy Award for her role. Fricker was widely recognized for her performances in My Left Foot and Home Alone 2. Her career spanned decades across film, television, and theatre productions. The world remembers her for her sincere and emotionally rich acting contributions.

title: 'Go to your MLA': BJP legislator's response to water crisis sparks protest in MP's Shivpuri
content: In an incident highlighting political disconnect, a BJP MLA disregarded the pleas of villagers at a public grievance meeting, insisting they were not his voters. In protest, the villagers placed a child atop the MLA’s vehicle to draw attention to their pleas concerning water and electricity shortages. The striking act of dissent has taken social media by storm, with the protest video circulating widely among users.

title: Not Okay To Call Someone 'Second-Class' Passenger: Supreme Court To Railways
content: The court linked the references to the "history of class divisions in our country" and called them "offensive to the spirit of the Constitution of India".

title: No NDA Entry Without Merger: Sources On BJP's Plan For Sharad Pawar's NCP
content: This development comes amid rumblings of political reunions in Maharashtra, fuelled by recent meetings between leaders from both NCP camps and key figures in the ruling dispensation.

title: Middle East On Boil Again? Iran Threatens "Zero Hour", Trump Deploys Planes
content: US President Donald Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room, where senior officials discussed several military options, such as attacking Iranian power plants and carrying out attacks against nuclear facilities to bury its uranium deeper underground.

title: PM Modi's "Vande Mataram" Message Set To Fly Into Space Tomorrow
content: As India's private space sector prepares for another defining moment, a special message from Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to ride into space aboard Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket.

title: Meghalaya To Table Resolution Opposing Uranium Mining
content: The Meghalaya government has announced that it would bring a resolution in the State Assembly to formally oppose and prohibit uranium mining, with Chief Minister Conrad K. Sangma asserting that his government has never given its approval for such activities in the state.

title: No Damage To Terminal In US Attacks On Chabahar Port: Centre
content: The Shahid Beheshti terminal at the Chabahar port in Iran has not faced any damage in US strikes on the key maritime gateway, India said on Friday.

title: 7.4-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Southern Mexico, Tsunami Alert Issued
content: A series of aftershocks, including some with magnitudes between 5 and 6, were also felt in Mexico, Guatemala and El Salvador.

title: How Is Bangladesh's PM Dealing With Student Protests
content: The Tarique Rahman government is keen to project a student-friendly image and does not want to be seen as clashing with students.

title: "Trying To Survive": Indians, Others Hit As Iran War Triggers Dubai Job Crisis
content: Months after the US-Israeli war with Iran spilt across the Gulf, many workers say jobs have disappeared, salaries have been cut and finding new employment has become increasingly difficult.

title: Girl, 7, Waiting To Go To School, Attacked By Stray Dogs In Maharashtra
content: A seven-year-old girl was injured after being attacked by a pack of stray dogs outside her home in Maharashtra's Gondia district, an incident captured on CCTV.

title: "At Variance With Facts": India Rejects Ex-Minister's Bullet Train Remarks
content: Japan has been playing a key role in implementation of the mega project in terms of financing it as well as sharing technology, and engineering expertise.

title: Minor's Parents "Sell" Her Baby After Rape, She Is Pregnant Again
content: According to police, the minor was lured and sexually assaulted on multiple occasions in 2025, resulting in pregnancy.

title: Internet Suspended In J&K's Doda After Man Killed, 2 Injured In Shooting
content: Internet services were suspended in Jammu and Kashmir's Doda district after a civilian was killed and two others were injured in a shooting incident, officials said.

title: Pakistan Army Kills 24 Taliban, Baloch Militants During Raids, Weapons Seized
content: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants in operations carried out over the previous day that killed 24 members of the outlawed Pakistani Taliban and Baloch separatist groups.

title: Maharashtra State Transport Bus Fares To Be Hiked By 13.5% From Tomorrow
content: MSRTC is one of the largest public bus service corporations in the country with a fleet of more than 14,000 buses. Daily more than 55 lakh passengers use its bus service.

title: "Jinnah Worshippers": Yogi Adityanath's Swipe At Congress, Samajwadi Party
content: Yogi Adityanath was in Shamli district for the inauguration and foundation-laying ceremony for 89 development projects worth over Rs 581 crore.

title: SpiceJet Fined Rs 1 Lakh By Consumer Protection Body Over 'Dark Patterns'
content: The CCPA said the authority had found that such practices "impair consumer autonomy, undermine informed decision-making and are inconsistent with the principles of fair and transparent consumer engagement".

title: Murshidabad Train Accident Updates: 3 Students Among 4 Killed In Train-Van Collision In West Bengal's Murshidabad
content: Murshidabad Train Accident Live Updates: The incident reportedly occured at 7 am, when students were headed to the school.

title: Sub-Inspector's Body Found With Blood On Clothes Inside Parked Car In Lucknow
content: Based on an identity card found in his pocket during the search, the man was identified as Sub-Inspector Ajay Thakur, posted at the Ghazipur police station.

title: Court Junks FIR Against Actor Shraddha Kapoor's Brother In 2022 Drug Case
content: The case dates back to June 2022, when Bengaluru Police detained Siddhanth Kapoor and a few others during a raid at a party in the city over allegations of drug consumption.

title: Andy Burnham set to be new UK PM, will take over from Keir Starmer on Monday
content: he was the only contender in the leadership contest to replace departing Prime Minister Keir Starmer . he secured nominations from 379 of the 403 Labour lawmakers in the house of commons . the former mayor of Greater Manchester has been prime minister-in-waiting for weeks .

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: 'Gandhi' film not on Mahatma’: Aamir faces political backlash for remarks on Sonam
content: aamir Khan's remarks on Sonam Wangchuk sparks political backlash . he says neither he nor director knew him when they made '3 idiots' . political parties accuse him of being unwilling to take a stand out of fear of the ruling establishment .

title: 'Who is D nexT': Iran billboard targets Trump after Graham's death
content: the billboard features the name of recently deceased US Senator Lindsey Graham . it features what appears to be a hidden reference to assasination threats for president . conservative voices in Iran have portrayed Graham's death as divine punishment .

title: Watch: Deadly landslide strikes China, dozens remain missing
content: the landslide struck at 9.08 am in Pengshui Miao and Tujia Autonomous County . despite the early warning, huge volumes of rock and mud crashed down the mountainside . authorities said 18 people had been rescued, including two with serious injuries .

title: Skyroot’s rocket Vikram-1 will carry to space a special message from PM Modi
content: skyroot's Vikram-1 will fly to space at 11.30am on saturday . it will carry a message for the universe from Prime Minister Narendra Modi . the handwritten card has PM Modi’s signature and has the date 26-06-26 written in his writing .

title: Consumer panel fines SpiceJet for ‘deploying dark patterns on its booking platform’
content: the airline has been directed to permanently discontinue default-consent practices . the violations include: 'automatic enrolment into SpiceClub. Pre-selected consent for promotional communications. Deceptive interface design influencing consumer decisions'

title: 'Losing consciousness': AISA activists' health worsens on Day 20 of hunger strike at Jantar Mantar
content: three activists on indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar have seen their health deteriorate further on the 20th day of their protest . doctors advised immediate hospitalisation for one of them due to hypoglycaemia . aameen has developed very high uric acid levels and has lost nearly 14 per cent of his body weight .

title: Bridges, airport, railway station: US attacks civilian infrastructure in Iran
content: u.s. intensifies its renewed bombing campaign against Iran . at least five bridges in southern Iran were hit in the strikes . seven people were reported killed in attacks on bridges . an airport in Iranshahr, near the border with Pakistan, was also struck .
